title: SSSP Research Lab: Implementable Shortest-Path Algorithms Inspired by Sorting-Barrier SSSP Research
description:
A Python research repository exploring the question of how much ordering a single-source shortest-path algorithm really needs. It provides reliable reference implementations (Dijkstra with binary heaps, Dial buckets, and radix heaps), educational versions of delta-stepping, ALT, contraction hierarchies, and Bellman-Ford, and experimental scaffolds inspired by frontier results on breaking the sorting barrier for directed SSSP, Thorup's linear-time integer SSSP, and near-linear negative-weight SSSP.

An optional PyO3/maturin Rust extension accelerates CSR-based Dijkstra and Dial kernels while the Python implementations remain the correctness reference.
